title: Discharge Barrier Resolution Workflow
description: A hospital-operations and compliance workflow that enforces control checks while allocating operational resources and closing governance loops. Focus case: discharge barrier resolution workflow.
assumptions: Resources (transport, equipment, vendors) are modelled as discrete unary resources and become unavailable while allocated to a case.
Sign-offs, documents, and authorizations are atomic predicates representing completed institutional checks.
Care-team and clinician roles are available in roster; availability predicates control assignment operations.
Interventions modelled are deterministic atomic operations (no probabilistic failures captured).
risks: Resource contention: limited transport/equipment can delay resolution if not prioritized.
Incomplete documentation or missing authorizations may block final closure despite other tasks completed.
Coordination failure between specialty services, pharmacy, and transport may create cycle dependencies requiring manual escalation.
Single-point actions (clinician approval) may become bottlenecks and cause throughput degradation.
Model does not capture timing or patient clinical deterioration risk during prolonged resolution; temporal constraints must be managed externally.
